name: Command A Translate (08-2025)
tagline: Cohere's enterprise machine-translation model — 111B params, 23 languages, open weights for research.
metaDescription: Command A Translate (08-2025) is Cohere's dedicated 111B-parameter machine-translation model covering 23 business languages, with an agentic Deep Translation m…
license: CC-BY-NC (non-commercial) + Cohere Lab's Acceptable Use Policy; commercial use via Cohere
overview: Command A Translate (08-2025) is Cohere's first dedicated machine-translation model, announced on August 28, 2025. Identified by the API name command-a-translate-08-2025, it is a 111-billion-parameter model built specifically to produce accurate, fluent translations rather than as a general-purpose chat assistant. Cohere positions it as an enterprise-grade, security-focused translation system and claims it leads other top models — including GPT-5, DeepSeek-V3, DeepL Pro's LLM, and Google Translate — across its supported business languages.

The model covers 23 languages: English, French, Spanish, Italian, German, Portuguese, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Arabic, Russian, Polish, Turkish, Vietnamese, Dutch, Czech, Indonesian, Ukrainian, Romanian, Greek, Hindi, Hebrew, and Persian. It has a 16K-token context window split as 8K input plus 8K output, and is optimized to run on just one or two A100/H100 GPUs, which makes self-hosted and private deployment practical for organizations that need full control over their data.

Beyond single-pass translation, Command A Translate adds Deep Translation — an agentic, multi-step refinement workflow that iteratively improves a draft translation before delivery to raise quality on complex content. The weights are openly published on Hugging Face under the CohereLabs organization for research use under a non-commercial CC-BY-NC license, while production and commercial deployment run through Cohere's Chat API or private deployments arranged with Cohere.
strengths: Purpose-built for translation across 23 business languages rather than retrofitted from a general chat model
Open weights published on Hugging Face (CohereLabs) for research, inspection, and fine-tuning
Deep Translation agentic refinement mode for higher quality on complex or nuanced text
Compact enough to run on 1-2 A100/H100 GPUs, enabling private and on-prem deployment
Enterprise focus on security and tenant control over translation workflows
useCases: High-volume localization of documents, support content, and product copy across 23 languages
Privately deployed translation for regulated or data-sensitive enterprises
Fine-tuning a translation model on domain-specific terminology and style
Agentic, multi-pass translation of complex content via Deep Translation
Research and benchmarking of open-weight machine-translation systems

Cohere changelog — Announcing Command A Translate (2025-08-28)
Slator — Cohere targets enterprise AI translation

architecture: Dense, auto-regressive decoder-only Transformer (111B parameters). Uses an optimized attention layout: three layers with sliding-window attention (window size 4096) plus RoPE for efficient local context, and a fourth layer with global attention (no positional embeddings) for full-sequence interaction. Trained with supervised fine-tuning and preference training; BF16 weights.

faq.a: It is Cohere's dedicated machine-translation model, announced on August 28, 2025, with the API name command-a-translate-08-2025. It is a 111-billion-parameter model built specifically to translate between 23 business languages, distinct from Cohere's general-purpose Command A chat models.

23 languages: English, French, Spanish, Italian, German, Portuguese, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Arabic, Russian, Polish, Turkish, Vietnamese, Dutch, Czech, Indonesian, Ukrainian, Romanian, Greek, Hindi, Hebrew, and Persian.

faq.q: Are the weights open, and can I use it commercially?
faq.a: The weights are published openly on Hugging Face under the CohereLabs organization, but under a non-commercial CC-BY-NC license with an acceptable-use addendum. Commercial and production use is handled through Cohere's Chat API or a private deployment arranged with Cohere.

Deep Translation is an agentic, multi-step refinement workflow that iteratively improves a draft translation before it is delivered, aimed at boosting quality on complex or high-stakes content. It is offered for enterprise use.
